title: Roughness-robust surface altermagnetism in $PT$ antiferromagnets
abstract: Surface altermagnetism extends spin splitting beyond bulk altermagnets through symmetry reduction at surfaces and interfaces. An existing classification applies to the local symmetry of atomically flat surface terraces. The present paper addresses the symmetry of macroscopic spin-momentum correlations that survive averaging over compensated rough surfaces. These correlations are governed by the surface antisymmetry Laue point group. Rough-surface altermagnetism is forbidden at any surface of a magnet whose antisymmetry space group contains antitranslations, and the classification therefore reduces to $PT$-symmetric antiferromagnets. By restoring all symmetries leaving the surface normal invariant, roughness can generate compensated surface altermagnets from uncompensated flat terminations, increase the surface symmetry, or suppress spin splitting. By combining bulk switchability with altermagnetic surface transport properties, roughness-robust surface altermagnetism in $PT$-symmetric antiferromagnets provides a route toward spintronic functionality.
summary: This paper develops a symmetry-based framework to classify surface altermagnetism that remains robust even on rough crystal surfaces. It proves that this phenomenon is restricted to PT-symmetric antiferromagnets by analyzing the Surface Antisymmetry Laue Point Group (SALPG). The work provides a detailed classification scheme linking bulk symmetries and surface orientation to observable magnetic transport properties.
main_problem: The study addresses the symmetry governing macroscopic spin-momentum correlations (altermagnetism) that persist when averaging over compensated, rough surfaces, moving beyond flat terrace approximations.
main_result: Roughness-robust surface altermagnetism is restricted to PT-symmetric antiferromagnets, whose symmetries are classified by the Surface Antisymmetry Laue Point Group (SALPG).
method: The analysis employs symmetry group theory, specifically deriving the SALPG from the bulk Antisymmetry Point Group (APG) using the little group of the surface normal.
model_or_system: The system under study is PT-symmetric antiferromagnets (PT-AFMs), focusing on how surface termination and roughness affect magnetic symmetry properties. The theory models macroscopic spin-momentum correlations surviving averaging over rough surfaces.
key_observables: Altermagnetic classes (e.g., d-wave, SD-SAM) determined by the SALPG; spin splitting magnitude and direction.
important_parameters: The surface normal vector ($\hat{n}$); the bulk Antisymmetry Point Group (APG).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes nonrelativistic properties and that macroscopic responses are averaged over an unbiased ensemble of rough surfaces, which restricts the phenomenon to PT-AFMs.
figures_summary: Table I provides a comprehensive classification linking SALPGs, altermagnetic classes, bulk APGs, and surface normals for PT-AFMs.
paper_structure: The paper first establishes that roughness averaging necessitates using the SALPG. It then derives constraints showing that only PT-AFMs support this robust effect. Finally, it provides a detailed classification table linking symmetry groups to observable altermagnetic states.
why_interesting: While focused on condensed matter magnetism, the rigorous application of group theory (point/space groups) to determine emergent macroscopic properties from averaging over disordered geometries is highly relevant to understanding open quantum systems and boundary effects in AMO physics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
